summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on two variance requests (BZA 2025-001 and BZA 2025-002) for a single property on State Route 48. The applicant, Victor Capitula, requested a variance to create a panhandle lot in an R3 zoning district (panhandle lots are only permitted by right in R1) and a front yard setback reduction from 50 feet to 35 feet on the newly created rear lot. Both variances were approved unanimously 5-0 with no public opposition.
decisions: BZA 2025-001: Approved as submitted - area variance to allow a panhandle lot in an R3 zoning district for parcel 112207K105 (5-0)
BZA 2025-002: Approved - front yard setback reduction from 50 feet to 35 feet on the rear split lot of parcel 112207K105 based on findings of fact (5-0)
Approval of video minutes from January 27th meeting (5-0)
action_items: Applicant Victor Capitula to follow up with township ladies/staff regarding approvals
Zoning staff to send applications to BZA members once received for potential March 17th cases (deadline February 27th for applications)
key_amounts: Front lot after split approximately 12,875 square feet
Panhandle/flag lot after split approximately 18,289 square feet
R3 minimum lot area: 12,800 square feet
Front yard setback reduced from 50 feet to 35 feet
notable_topics: Area variance for panhandle lot creation in R3 zoning district (BZA 2025-001)
Front yard setback reduction variance from 50 to 35 feet (BZA 2025-002)
Lot split of parcel 112207K105 on State Route 48
Discussion of zoning code change from previous code allowing panhandle lots in all districts to 2017 code restricting to R1
Findings of fact review criteria for BZA variances
attendees: Victor Capitula (applicant, 5327 Cali Hill Road, Milford, OH 45150)
notes: Mr. Langenheim was absent. There was some procedural confusion during the second variance vote where the initial motion had to be retracted and remade to include reference to findings of fact. No public attendees were present to speak for or against the variances. Three neighbors had called the zoning office with questions but expressed no objections. The meeting ran from approximately 6:30 PM to 7:07 PM. Staff noted there is a 45-acre property nearby being cleared that neighbors were curious about, but the township has no information on that activity.
title: BZA 2-24-25
